prd.summary: Reusable reply templates with auto-fill variables inside the seller inbox, enabling solo shop owners to send consistent answers to repetitive buyer questions in two clicks.
prd.problem_statement: Solo shop owners answer the same five questions (shipping time, sizing, customization, returns, delays) dozens of times per week. Today they copy-paste from Notes or retype from memory, which is slow, error-prone, and produces inconsistent answers that appear in negative reviews.

target_user.situation: Handling 20+ buyer conversations per day and fielding repetitive questions about shipping, sizing, customization, returns, and delays

in_scope.item: Templates drawer in inbox UI with save-as-template and insert-template actions
in_scope.rationale: Tests whether sellers will adopt the core workflow (capture and reuse) and whether two-click insertion is fast enough to displace copy-paste behavior.

in_scope.item: Auto-fill variables for buyer name and order number only
in_scope.rationale: Minimizes implementation scope for a two-person team in one release cycle; tests whether basic personalization is sufficient to avoid 'impersonal' perception.

in_scope.item: Five starter templates covering the five most-cited repetitive questions (shipping, sizing, customization, returns, delays)
in_scope.rationale: Reduces friction for first-time users and validates that the problem is real; sellers can immediately measure time saved against their current workflow.

out_of_scope.item: Custom variables beyond buyer name and order number (e.g., shop name, product SKU, shipping carrier)
out_of_scope.reason: Scope creep for a two-person team; each new variable adds UI, backend, and testing complexity.
out_of_scope.revisit_when: After measuring adoption of basic templates and identifying the next-highest-value variable from usage data.

out_of_scope.reason: Premature organization; five starter templates do not require categorization. Adds UI complexity that delays launch.
out_of_scope.revisit_when: When the seller has created 10+ custom templates and reports difficulty finding them.

out_of_scope.reason: Constraint specifies web platform only; mobile adds platform-specific overhead. Sellers can use web on mobile browser.
out_of_scope.revisit_when: After web launch stabilizes and mobile usage is measured via analytics.

out_of_scope.reason: Introduces moderation, privacy, and legal complexity. Not validated as a user need yet.
out_of_scope.revisit_when: After interviews reveal sellers asking for this or competitive pressure emerges.

out_of_scope.item: Analytics dashboard and template performance insights
out_of_scope.reason: Nice-to-have instrumentation; does not unblock core value. Telemetry for success metric can be added post-launch.
out_of_scope.revisit_when: After MVP launch, if the team wants to optimize template content or measure ROI.

success_metrics.metric: Share of replies sent via template among sellers active in week 2
Seller retention (active in week 6 vs. week 1) among template users
Net Promoter Score or qualitative feedback on template experience (impersonality concern)

risks.risk: Sellers perceive templated replies as impersonal or brand-damaging in a 'handmade' marketplace, leading to low adoption or negative buyer feedback.
risks.mitigation: Conduct a small qualitative test (5–10 sellers) in week 1: have them send 2–3 templated replies and ask buyers for feedback on tone. If buyers flag impersonality, pivot to a 'template + personal note' UI before full rollout.

risks.risk: Five starter templates do not match the actual top questions sellers receive, reducing perceived relevance and adoption.
risks.mitigation: Validate the five questions against the 12 seller interviews and forum threads before finalizing template text. If interviews reveal different top questions, rewrite templates to match.

risks.risk: Auto-fill variables fail or insert wrong data (e.g., buyer name from previous order), eroding trust in the feature.
risks.mitigation: Implement unit tests for variable substitution and a manual QA pass on 20 templated replies before launch. Add a preview step before send so sellers can verify correctness.

open_questions: Riskiest assumption (proposed): Buyers will not perceive templated replies as impersonal. How will you validate this before full rollout?
What are the exact five questions from the 12 seller interviews? (Needed to finalize starter template text.)
Will sellers be able to edit a template after inserting it, or must they send as-is?
next_release: Custom variables (SKU, shipping carrier, shop name) once usage data identifies the next-highest-value variable.
Template categories and search when sellers have created 10+ custom templates.
Mobile app support after web launch stabilizes.

flags.issue: Primary metric measured too early; week-2 adoption inflated by novelty.
flags.why_it_matters: A 40% share in week 2 may collapse by week 6 if templates don't solve the real problem or feel impersonal. You won't know if the feature actually works until repeat behavior stabilizes.
flags.suggested_fix: Move the primary metric to week 4 or week 6, or add a guardrail for week-6 retention to catch novelty collapse.

flags.issue: Evidence is strong but recency and sample details should be stated.
flags.why_it_matters: If interviews were conducted in June and it's now much later, market conditions may have shifted. Readers need to know how fresh the data is.
flags.suggested_fix: Add the current date or 'conducted in June' to the problem statement so the team can assess staleness during execution.

flags.issue: Starter templates are not validated; five questions may not match seller reality.
flags.why_it_matters: If the five starter templates don't address the actual top questions, sellers won't use them, and the 40% target will miss. You're shipping content you haven't validated.
flags.suggested_fix: Before finalizing template text, extract the exact five questions from the 12 interviews and forum threads and confirm they match the proposed templates.

critique.strongest_element: The riskiest assumption is explicit and testable: 'Buyers won't read templated replies as impersonal.' This shows the team has identified the core threat to adoption and can design a validation plan around it.
critique.weakest_element: The success metric is measured in week 2, which captures novelty adoption but not sustained behavior change or the impersonality risk. A 40% share in week 2 tells you nothing about whether the feature survives week 6.
